name: Limbu

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Limbu (limb1266).

notes: Skeleton (lexicon-dependent). Limbu is a Kiranti (Sino-Tibetan) language of eastern Nepal and Sikkim written in the Limbu (Sirijonga) script, an abugida with vowel signs and syllable-final consonant diacritics. Lexical tone, where present, is not orthographically recoverable. More fundamentally, grapheme-to-IPA for this script requires a per-word lexicon/pronunciation stratum that the engine does not bundle: syllable-final and stacked/sign-conditioned readings are not derivable letter-by-letter, so engine coverage is lexicon-dependent and effectively zero without that stratum. No grapheme map is asserted for this reason.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Limbu (limb1266)
